title: NOC 32102 – Paramedical Occupations | GTR Immigration
description: Paramedical professionals under NOC 32102 play a critical frontline role in emergency and pre-hospital care across Canada. With expanding emergency medical services, increasing healthcare demands, and workforce replacement needs, Paramedical Occupations continue to offer stable employment opportunities nationwide. As a TEER 2 occupation, paramedics and emergency medical professionals qualify under several economic immigration pathways, making this a structured and practical route toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER2 – College diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group32 – Technical occupations in healthSub-Major Group321 – Technical occupations in health (except practitioners of natural healing)Minor Group3210 – Technical occupations in therapy and assessmentNOC Code32102

duties: Paramedical professionals perform some or all of the following duties:
Assess trauma victims and medically ill patients
Provide pre-hospital emergency care including CPR and oxygen therapy
Administer medications and establish IV therapy
Immobilize fractures and provide wound care
Transport patients by air, land, or water
Collaborate with dispatch, hospitals, police, and fire services
Document patient conditions and treatment provided
Assist hospital personnel during patient handover
May supervise and train junior staff
employment_requirements: Completion of a one- to three-year college or recognized paramedical program
Licensing by a provincial regulatory body required in all provinces
Appropriate driver’s license required for operating emergency vehicles
Internationally trained paramedics must complete credential recognition and provincial licensing requirements before practice.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $44,300 to $86,600, depending on province, certification level (Primary, Advanced, Critical Care), and work setting.
job_outlook: The national outlook remains stable, with consistent demand for emergency medical professionals, particularly in:
Advanced and critical care paramedics with Canadian certification have stronger employment prospects and higher earning potential.
labour_market_demand: Although supply is slightly higher than demand nationally, regional shortages remain common, particularly in provinces such as Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Saskatchewan, and northern territories like Yukon.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 32102?
Provincial Nominee Program streams targeting healthcare workers
Strong job stability in public emergency services
Access to public healthcare and settlement benefits
Emergency medical services remain a core component of Canada’s healthcare infrastructure.
pathways: If you are a qualified paramedic or emergency medical professional, NOC 32102 offers a practical immigration pathway to Canada.
Eligibility assessment under Express Entry and PNP
